qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: LFS batch endpoint vulnerable to path traversal in object OID parameter
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding describes a path traversal vulnerability in pkg/web/git_lfs.go:107 and pkg/storage/local.go:83, while the advisory describes an authorization bypass in pkg/ssh/cmd/import.go and pkg/backend/repo.go:102. Different vulnerable code locations, different vulnerability type (path traversal vs authorization bypass), different attack vector (LFS batch HTTP API vs SSH repo import command), and different root cause (validation order vs missing authorization check).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: Neither qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes an authorization bypass in the SSH repo import command (pkg/ssh/cmd/import.go, pkg/backend/repo.go:102) that allows authenticated users to clone server-local private repositories. Finding 'example' describes a deserialization vulnerability in JavaScript code, which is a different language, codebase, and vulnerability type. Finding 'lfs_batch_path_traversal' describes a path traversal vulnerability in the LFS batch API (pkg/web/git_lfs.go:107), which is a different vulnerable code location, different vulnerability type, and different attack vector from the advisory.
evidence: Advisory vulnerable code: pkg/ssh/cmd/import.go, pkg/ssh/cmd/cmd.go:172, pkg/backend/user.go:94, pkg/backend/repo.go:102
Advisory vulnerability type: Authorization bypass allowing cloning of server-local private repositories
Advisory attack vector: SSH repo import command with filesystem path as remote parameter
Finding 'example' vulnerable code: src/routes/import.js, src/jobs/processImport.js, src/lib/serialization/loadArchive.js
Finding 'example' vulnerability type: Unsafe deserialization leading to RCE
Finding 'lfs_batch_path_traversal' vulnerability type: Path traversal in LFS batch API OID parameter
No overlap in vulnerable code locations between advisory and findings
Different root cause mechanisms: missing authorization check vs deserialization vs validation order
